title: cerradicola lamprophylla
intro: Cerradicola lamprophylla, also known as the "sensitive plant," is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). It is a tropical plant native to Central and South America, known for its compound leaves that fold up when touched or disturbed, a defense mechanism to protect itself from herbivores. The leaves and stems of Cerradicola lamprophylla contain flavonoids, which have been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. Herbal preparations of Cerradicola lamprophylla, such as teas and infusions, have been traditionally used to treat digestive problems, reduce fever, and promote wound healing.
